issues.title: Ability to disable entire model provider with property
issues.body:
**Expected Behavior**

Have properties to enable or disable an entire model. For example, if autoconfigurations for both OpenAI and Azure OpenAI are on the classpath, Azure OpenAI can be selected by setting `spring.ai.openai.enabled: false`, or opposite with `spring.ai.azure.openai.enabled: false`.

**Current Behavior**

Only individual models can be disabled by property. Disabling all of openai means disabling chat, embeddings, image, etc, with more coming as they add new features.

Alternatively, normal `spring.autoconfigure.exclude` can be used, but this property is awkward for being a CSV list making it hard to merge configs, and it also requires knowing the classname which is not as intuitive as a well-defined propertly.

While a user could exclude the autoconfigure artifact from classpath, it seems useful to be able to build applications that can select their model provider at runtime in an easy way, without rebuilding binaries.

**Context**

I am trying to write some currently relatively simple agent code that can be used with either OpenAi or Azure OpenAI through simple configuration tweaks.

comments.body:
In the M7 release we separated out the autoconfiguration to make it more granular.  You can now opt out individually from each feature.  So using 

```
spring:
  ai:
    model:
      embedding: false
```

will disable autoconfig for embedding models.



See https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/embeddings/azure-openai-embeddings.html#_embedding_properties and let me know if this works for you.

Supporting a mixture of openai models, embeddings, etc is an area we are working on improving further.  Thanks for the feedback.

Going to close, please reopen if this doesn't help you - or help as much as you would like in your use case.
